data.title: Bitcoin Faces Challenges Amid Yen Intervention Concerns
data.description: Bitcoin is currently underperforming compared to gold as concerns about potential yen intervention impact risk assets worldwide. According to NS3.AI, the uncertainty surrounding currency intervention is prompting investors to turn to safer assets such as gold. Market participants are exercising caution due to the unclear outlook for risk-on investments.

data.title: Mitsui Sumitomo Trust Holdings Reveals Investment in Bitcoin Treasury Firm
data.description: Japan's fifth-largest bank, Mitsui Sumitomo Trust Holdings, has disclosed its investment in the Bitcoin treasury company, MicroStrategy (MSTR). According to Foresight News, the bank holds 606,629 shares of MicroStrategy, valued at $96.6 million. The total assets of Mitsui Sumitomo Trust Holdings amount to $633 billion.

data.title: Two Ethereum stakers withdrew 13,000 ETH from Binance.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, two Ethereum staker wallets withdrew 13,000 ETH from Binance, worth $37.64 million.

data.title: Silver prices surged briefly and are currently trading at $110.5 per ounce, with New York silver futures up 9% on the day.
data.description: Spot silver rose $0.60 in the short term, currently trading at $110.50 per ounce; New York silver futures surged 9.00% intraday, currently trading at $110.47 per ounce. (Jinshi)

data.title: PBOC Focuses on Macroprudential Management System Development
data.description: The People's Bank of China (PBOC) recently convened a macroprudential work meeting for 2026, focusing on the advancement of a comprehensive macroprudential management system. According to NS3.AI, the bank intends to strengthen its financial stability functions, innovate policy tools, and broaden the scope of macroprudential policies to proactively identify systemic financial risks. Furthermore, the PBOC aims to enhance the cross-border use of the RMB, optimize clearing bank networks, and bolster Shanghai's position as an international financial center.

data.title: Gold Gains Favor as Portfolio Hedge Over Long-Term Bonds
data.description: Gold is increasingly being favored over long-term government bonds as a hedge against stock market risk, according to NS3.AI. This shift is attributed to recent simultaneous sell-offs in both equities and long-term bonds, which have shaken investor confidence in bonds as dependable hedges. The trend indicates a broader reevaluation of risk management strategies within investment portfolios.

data.title: Digital Asset Investment Products Experience Significant Outflows
data.description: CoinShares has reported a substantial outflow of $1.73 billion from digital asset investment products, marking the largest single-day outflow since mid-November 2025. According to Foresight News, the majority of these outflows were concentrated in the United States, amounting to nearly $1.8 billion. In contrast, Switzerland, Germany, and Canada saw inflows. Bitcoin and Ethereum led the outflows with $1.09 billion and $630 million, respectively, indicating a generally negative market sentiment. However, Solana experienced an inflow of $17.1 million, defying the overall trend.

data.title: Zimbabwe Seeks Membership in Expanding BRICS Bloc
data.description: Zimbabwe has officially applied to join the BRICS economic bloc, which currently includes 11 members following Indonesia's inclusion in early 2025. According to NS3.AI, the nation is awaiting feedback from existing member states, with Russia, South Africa, and Brazil showing support for Zimbabwe's bid. Additionally, more than 20 other countries, such as Bahrain, Malaysia, Turkey, and Vietnam, have submitted applications as BRICS continues to grow, providing an alternative to Western-dominated financial systems.

data.title: eToro: Gold is replacing traditional stable, low-risk assets as a portfolio hedging tool
data.description: eToro analyst Lale Akoner points out that as investors seek new ways to hedge equity risk, gold is increasingly replacing traditional stable, low-risk assets—long-term government bonds—as a portfolio allocation choice. "Historically, duration exposure has helped buffer against declines in risky assets. However, the recent simultaneous sell-off in both stocks and long-term bonds has eroded investor confidence in bonds as a reliable hedging tool in critical moments," she says. She suggests that investors are therefore replacing bond exposure with gold in their portfolios, indicating a broader rethinking of portfolio risk management. (Jinshi)

data.title: Yi Lihua: There is no God's-eye view in investment and trading; buy and sell strategies should be determined based on medium- to long-term timelines.
data.description: Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua posted on the X platform: "Everyone is puzzled. We are buying heavily, BMNR and MicroStrategy are buying, and CZ is calling for a super bull market cycle, but the price of cryptocurrencies remains weak and volatile. What is the reason? After all, the stock market and gold/silver have gone crazy. In our view, there are several main reasons: one is the impact of the four-year cycle and the October 11 crash; another is the impact of the yen interest rate hike; another is the lack of new purchases by the US BTC strategic reserve; another is the short sellers taking advantage of this opportunity to dump the market; and another is the safe-haven funds in gold, silver, and the stock market. However, analyzing from the opposite perspective, even with so many negative factors, ETH is still stable around $3,000, fluctuating and consolidating. This is why we decided to build a position after clearing our position at $4,500. Many people suggested that we wait and buy, as there will be a better entry point. However, there is no God's-eye view in investment trading. It is difficult to know what the lowest point is. The difference between investment and speculation is that it is difficult to do short-term trading. Even if there are considerable floating profits, we do not move. We determine our buying and selling strategies based on medium- to long-term timeframes."

data.title: The People's Bank of China held its 2026 Macroprudential Work Conference, conducting a forward-looking assessment of potential systemic financial risks.
data.description: The People's Bank of China (PBOC) convened its 2026 Macroprudential Work Conference. The conference emphasized that macroprudential work in 2026 should continue to follow the deployment of building a comprehensive macroprudential management system, strengthening the central bank's macroprudential management functions, continuously improving the working mechanism of the Macroprudential and Financial Stability Committee, gradually expanding the coverage of macroprudential policies, proactively assessing potential systemic financial risks, innovating and enriching the policy toolbox, and maintaining the sound operation of the financial market and the overall stability of the financial system. Guided by serving the construction of a new development pattern, the conference called for further improving policies on the cross-border use of RMB, promoting trade facilitation, optimizing the layout of RMB clearing banks, better leveraging the role of currency swaps, developing the offshore RMB market, supporting the construction of Shanghai as an international financial center, and better meeting the needs of various entities for RMB transaction settlement, investment and financing, and risk management. (Jinshi)

data.title: Smart Money Address Accumulates 20,000 ETH, Sees Immediate Gains
data.description: A smart money address has recently acquired 20,000 ETH through Wintermute, according to Foresight News. This transaction, valued at approximately $56.13 million, was executed at a cost of around $2,806.58 per ETH. Following the purchase, the price of ETH rebounded, resulting in an unrealized profit of $2.101 million for the address. Since January 21, the address has accumulated a total of 70,013 ETH, with an average cost of about $2,907.69 per ETH.

data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, a whale recently deposited 7.006 million FARTCO tokens into Coinbase. These tokens represent a loss of approximately $135,000 if sold, based on what it had accumulated five days prior.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Faces Pressure Amid Macroeconomic Concerns
data.description:
Cryptocurrencies experienced significant pressure during the early Asian trading session on January 26, leading to the liquidation of over $550 million in leveraged long positions, with Bitcoin briefly dipping to $86,000. According to PANews, safe-haven assets like gold and silver continued their upward trend. The heightened risk aversion is attributed to several macroeconomic concerns, including U.S. President Donald Trump's suggestion of imposing a 100% tariff on Canadian imports, the rising risk of a partial U.S. government shutdown as current funding expires on January 30, and uncertainties surrounding potential U.S.-Japan currency market interventions to stabilize the yen. The New York Federal Reserve's "rate check" on the dollar/yen on Friday further heightened market caution regarding yen depreciation.

In response to these macroeconomic uncertainties, the crypto derivatives market has adopted a defensive stance, with an increase in put skew and implied volatility, alongside significant capital flows shifting long-term put positions to lower strike prices. Looking ahead, with a dense macroeconomic event calendar, including tech stock earnings and the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision, implied volatility is expected to remain elevated. Although the Federal Reserve is anticipated to keep rates unchanged, the market will closely watch Chairman Powell's guidance on future policy directions. Until multiple risks, particularly the government shutdown risk, are clarified, cryptocurrency prices may exhibit volatility in the near term.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Potential Four-Month Losing Streak Amid January Options Expiry
data.description: Bitcoin is poised to encounter a rare four-month losing streak, a situation not observed since 2018. According to NS3.AI, this downward trend aligns with the January options expiry, which could influence short-term price fluctuations. Investors are advised to exercise caution, as this combination may heighten market volatility in the coming period.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin may face its first four-month consecutive decline since 2018.
data.description: Bitcoin is poised for its fourth consecutive month of decline, a scenario unseen since the six-month losing streak of 2018-2019. Despite having one full trading week remaining in January, Bitcoin has already seen a slight dip, hovering around $87,000. Bitcoin previously recorded monthly declines in October, November, and December, representing significant pullbacks from its all-time high in October, with a drop of approximately 36% from peak to trough during this period. Even during the 2022 bear market, when Bitcoin's price fell from $69,000 to $15,000, it never experienced more than three consecutive months of monthly declines. However, despite the weak spot price, the derivatives market is showing some optimism. Deribit data shows that the notional value of call options with a strike price of $100,000 is the highest, approaching $900 million, indicating that a significant number of traders are betting on a price rebound to six-figure levels. (CoinDesk)
